DEMURRER to 4TH Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any attorney, any party or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SIU CHAN, JUSTIN TIN DEMURRER to 4TH Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend. Demurrer to the causes of action for breach of contract, open account, and unjust enrichment is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in clear and concise language facts giving rise to these causes of action, including but not limited to: (1) the "final" terms of the contract they are suing on; (2) are they alleging a breach of 3 separate contracts or one contract (what are the exact final terms of the one contract); (3) are they seeking a reformation of Contract B (allegations as to the content of Contract B are contrary to the plain language of the contract attached) and exactly what reformation is being sought; (4) facts to remove the contract/contract from the statute of frauds and statute of limitations.
Demurer to the cause of action for elder abuse is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action as to the moving defendant.
Plaintiff to identify elements of each of these causes of action and allege facts in support of each element as to each defendant. All new allegations shall be in bold font.
Plaintiffs' request for leave to amend to add causes of action/allegations unrelated to the demurrer is DENIED at this time. Any such request shall be made by a properly noticed motion.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT SIU CHAN, JUSTIN TIN DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the causes of action for breach of contract, open account, and unjust enrichment is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in clear and concise language facts giving rise to these causes of action, including but not limited to: (1) what are the terms of the contract they are suing on; (2) are they alleging a breach of 3 separate contracts or one contract (what are the exact final terms of the one contract); (3) are they seeking a reformation of Contract B (allegations as to the content of Contract B are contrary to the plain language of the contract attached); (4) facts to remove the contract/contract from the statute of frauds and statute of limitations.
Demurer to the cause of action for elder abuse is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action as to the moving defendant.
Demurrer to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is OVERRULED. All elements of this cause of action are sufficiently alleged.
All amendments to be limited to the scope of the demurrer. No additional amendments allowed, e.g. new parties, new causes of action, etc. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
